rulings.Case Title: HSBC BANK USA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS TRUSTEE FOR VS. KEYHAN MOHANNA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 12, 2021 line 1. PLAINTIFF HSBC BANK USA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION's FOR MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES is continued to June 16, 2021 for supplemental briefing for the moving party to provide a supplemental declaration attaching a judgment awarding attorney's fees referenced in the moving papers: "This Court found Plaintiff to be the prevailing party in the underlying action and issued judgment and attorneys' fees in its favor." (Moving papers 7:3-4). Supplemental declaration shall be limited to the introduction of the judgment and/or order awarding fees with award of fees section highlighted. Declaration to be filed and served no later than June 1, 2021. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 6315 THIRD STREET, LLC VS. GREGORY HAYNES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 12, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT GREGORY HAYNES' DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to plead and disclose its alternative theories of the case. The Court takes judicial notice of the unlawful detainer claim and assertions made in that verified complaint. Such assertions are in direct contradiction to the claims in this action and are not disclosed in this action. While Plaintiff cites authorities for the proposition that plaintiff may plead inconsistent claims predicated on both existence and absence of an agreement (here, lease), Plaintiff fails to cite any authority that such claims may be asserted in different actions without any disclosure of Plaintiff's lack of certainty as to the facts in either case. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the declaratory relief cause of action. As Plaintiff argues in opposition declaratory relief is available in limited jurisdiction cases per CCP 580(b)(4) as authorized by CCP 86. Plaintiff does not explain how this case falls within allowable cases under CCP 86(a)(7). To the extent defendant demurs to the cause of action for trespass based on unavailability of injunctive relief, demurrer is OVERRULED. Defendant cites no authority for the proposition that demurrer is a proper way to address one of several requests for relief in a cause of action. The Court however notes that while Plaintiff admits that permanent injunction is not available in limited jurisdiction cases (Opposition 9:14-15) Plaintiff nevertheless prayed for permanent injunction (Complaint 7:14) and did not amend/withdraw this prayer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
